articles.title: Universal Kids Resort Opens in Frisco, Texas
articles.summary: Universal's dedicated children's resort has officially opened in Frisco, Texas after three years of development, featuring seven themed areas built around beloved family characters and stories. The project represents a major new benchmark for family-focused entertainment destinations in the US. FEC operators should watch this closely as it sets new expectations for themed, immersive family experiences in the competitive Texas market.

articles.title: Launch Family Entertainment Advances Growth with Smaller-Format Park Model in H1 2026
articles.summary: Launch Family Entertainment introduced a smaller-format franchise park model in the first half of 2026, giving franchisees more flexibility when evaluating real estate opportunities. The move signals a strategic shift toward scalable, accessible FEC footprints that can penetrate markets previously too small for traditional builds. This is highly relevant for prospective FEC franchisees and investors weighing entry costs and location options.

articles.title: Enchanted Parks Deploys WhiteWater's Vantage Platform Across Eight Former Six Flags Sites
articles.summary: Enchanted Parks has selected WhiteWater's Vantage platform to unify ticketing, payments, loyalty, F&B, retail, access control, and mobile engagement across eight US parks in one of the most ambitious guest-tech integrations in the attractions industry. The rollout aims to deliver a frictionless, personalized, data-driven visitor experience at scale. FEC operators of all sizes should take note of how integrated technology ecosystems are becoming a core competitive differentiator.

articles.title: How to Build a Profitable Laser Tag Business: Lessons from Operators
articles.summary: A detailed operational guide explores what separates thriving laser tag businesses from struggling ones, drawing on real-world experience including a Connecticut operator who pivoted to a fully mobile model after the pandemic. Key lessons cover choosing the right business model, systems selection, and crafting a strong customer experience from day one. Highly actionable reading for any FEC operator considering adding or expanding laser tag.

articles.title: The Breakers Resort Plans New Family Entertainment Center in Palm Beach
articles.summary: Renderings have been released for a planned Family Entertainment Center at The Breakers, the iconic Palm Beach luxury resort, signaling growing interest in FEC concepts within upscale hospitality settings. The project illustrates how traditional hospitality venues are increasingly incorporating FEC-style amenities to broaden family appeal. Operators and designers should watch this trend as it opens new partnership and design opportunities.

articles.title: Five Iron Golf Launches London Location as Part of £20M UK Expansion
articles.summary: US-based indoor golf and entertainment concept Five Iron Golf has opened its first London venue as part of a £20 million UK expansion plan. The move underscores the continued international appetite for experiential, tech-driven indoor entertainment formats beyond traditional bowling and arcades. FEC operators and investors should monitor Five Iron's growth trajectory as a case study in scaling premium indoor entertainment across new markets.

articles.title: New Waterpark and Entertainment Complex Planned for West Des Moines
articles.summary: New plans have been revealed for a large-scale waterpark and entertainment complex in West Des Moines, with the city subsequently approving a development agreement for the Grand Experience project. The development represents a significant mixed-use FEC investment in the Midwest and offers insight into how municipalities are partnering with entertainment developers. FEC investors and developers should track this as a model for public-private leisure development.

articles.title: West Des Moines Approves Development Agreement for Grand Experience Entertainment Complex
articles.summary: The city of West Des Moines has formally approved a development agreement for the Grand Experience, a major waterpark and entertainment destination. City-backed agreements like this are increasingly important tools for FEC developers seeking to reduce financial risk on large-scale projects. This approval moves the project closer to groundbreaking and serves as a useful case study in municipal partnership for leisure development.

articles.title: Majid Al Futtaim Opens Large-Scale Explorers FEC at Dubai Shopping Mall
articles.summary: Majid Al Futtaim has launched Explorers, billed as Dubai's only large-scale indoor FEC combining adventure play with expert-led interactive workshops, at City Centre Mirdif mall. Designed for children aged 1-12, the venue spans three distinct themed zones and positions learning alongside physical play. The concept offers a compelling blueprint for mall-based FEC operators looking to differentiate through educational programming.

articles.title: LEVEL 99 to Open First Connecticut Location in West Hartford
articles.summary: LEVEL 99, the innovative adult-oriented indoor adventure attraction, is expanding to West Hartford, Connecticut, marking the brand's first location in the state. The concept, which features physically engaging game-based challenges for older teens and adults, continues to grow its footprint as demand for non-traditional FEC formats rises. Operators should monitor LEVEL 99's expansion as evidence of strong consumer appetite for activity-based entertainment beyond classic arcade and bowling formats.

articles.title: Market Research Breaks Down Key Segments Shaping the Family and Indoor Entertainment Center Industry
articles.summary: A new market analysis outlines the major segments driving growth in the family and indoor entertainment center industry, offering operators and investors a structured view of where demand is strongest. Understanding these segments is critical for FEC owners making decisions about concept mix, capital allocation, and market positioning. Operators should review this data to benchmark their offerings against broader industry trends.

articles.title: Sky Zone Trampoline Park Opens New Location, Creating Local Jobs
articles.summary: A new Sky Zone Trampoline Park has opened, bringing family entertainment options and new employment to the local community. Sky Zone continues to be one of the most active franchise FEC brands expanding across North America. The opening highlights ongoing consumer demand for active, trampoline-based entertainment venues.

articles.title: Eternity Bowling to Showcase EV99 String Bowling System at Fun Asia Expo 2026
articles.summary: Eternity Bowling will demonstrate its EV99 String Bowling System at the Fun Asia Expo 2026 in Indonesia, targeting FEC and bowling center operators across the Asia-Pacific region. String pinsetters continue to gain traction globally as a lower-maintenance, cost-efficient alternative to traditional free-fall systems. FEC operators considering adding or upgrading bowling should follow developments from this expo closely.

articles.title: Boxing Arcade Machines Gaining Popularity in Modern Entertainment Venues
articles.summary: A new report examines how boxing arcade machines are emerging as a revenue-generating attraction in FECs, arcades, and entertainment venues worldwide. The format appeals to a broad demographic and requires minimal floor space relative to its earning potential. FEC operators looking to diversify their arcade mix should consider the growing consumer interest in skill-and-strength arcade formats.

articles.title: RePlay Magazine Spotlights FatCats and Blazer Tag Operations Across America
articles.summary: RePlay Magazine features a look at FatCats and Blazer Tag venues as part of its Fun Across America series, highlighting operational and design approaches at multi-attraction FEC locations. These profiles offer useful benchmarks for operators running similar multi-concept venues. FEC owners can glean practical insights on attraction mix, theming, and customer flow from these real-world examples.

articles.title: Zamania by Leo & Loona FEC Opens at Ibn Battuta Mall in Dubai
articles.summary: Zamania by Leo & Loona, a new themed family entertainment center, has opened at Ibn Battuta Mall in Dubai, further intensifying competition in the Gulf region's booming FEC market. The venue adds to a growing cluster of mall-anchored FEC concepts targeting families in the UAE. Operators and developers eyeing Middle East expansion should take note of the increasingly crowded but high-demand landscape.

articles.title: Massanutten Resort Debuts Virginia's First Alpine Coaster, 'Mountain Mayhem'
articles.summary: Massanutten Resort in Virginia's Shenandoah Valley has opened Mountain Mayhem, the state's first alpine coaster, as part of its Family Adventure Park offering. Alpine coasters are increasingly popular add-on attractions at resorts and outdoor FECs due to their all-season appeal and strong revenue-per-rider metrics. This opening is a useful case study for family resort operators evaluating low-footprint, high-impact ride additions.

articles.title: Momentum Leisure Rebrands Polish Indoor Parks from Majaland to Episodia
articles.summary: Dutch-Polish operator Momentum Leisure is retiring the Majaland brand across its three Polish indoor parks and relaunching under the new Episodia identity, designed as a future-proof platform for multiple characters, stories, and worlds. The rebrand follows 12 months of strategic development and aims to move beyond licensed IP dependency toward a proprietary universe. FEC operators reliant on licensed characters should watch this as a case study in building brand equity independent of third-party IP.

articles.title: InfoComm 2026: AV Industry Prioritizes Simplicity and Ease of Operation
articles.summary: InfoComm 2026 revealed a shift in the audiovisual industry toward making complex systems simpler to deploy, operate, and maintain, even as raw performance specs continue to improve. This trend has direct implications for FEC operators who rely on AV technology for immersive attractions, digital signage, and guest engagement systems. Easier-to-manage AV infrastructure could reduce staffing requirements and lower operational costs for entertainment venues.

articles.title: Alterface and Fantawild Partner to Develop Interactive Attractions
articles.summary: Interactive attraction technology specialist Alterface has formed a partnership with Chinese theme park giant Fantawild to co-develop new interactive ride and attraction experiences. Alterface's shooting and interactive systems are widely used in dark rides and FEC attractions globally. This partnership could accelerate innovation in interactive attraction technology with potential downstream benefits for FEC operators seeking engaging new ride formats.

articles.title: Heat Wave Drives Families to Indoor Entertainment Venues in Greensboro
articles.summary: A summer heat wave in Greensboro, North Carolina is pushing families toward indoor entertainment businesses, providing a measurable revenue boost for FECs and similar venues. The trend highlights the importance of indoor attractions as weather-proof alternatives during extreme heat events, which are becoming more frequent. FEC operators should consider marketing strategies that capitalize on heat wave periods as high-demand windows.

articles.title: Exhibition Hub and Fever Refresh Van Gogh Immersive Experience for Second Cincinnati Run
articles.summary: Exhibition Hub and Fever have relaunched a refreshed edition of Van Gogh: The Immersive Experience in Cincinnati, demonstrating that touring immersive experiences can be successfully renewed for repeat-market audiences with updated content. The strategy of extending an experience's lifecycle through content refresh rather than full replacement offers a cost-effective model for immersive attraction operators. FECs and entertainment venue operators exploring immersive touring shows should note this approach.

articles.title: Fiesta Village FEC in Colton, California Closes After Final Weekend
articles.summary: Crowds gathered for a final round of fun at Fiesta Village, a longtime family entertainment center in Colton, California, before its permanent closure. The venue's closing reflects ongoing pressures facing independent, older-format FECs competing against newer experiential concepts. Operators should view this as a reminder of the importance of continuous reinvestment and concept evolution to maintain relevance.

articles.title: Parky's FEC at Moorestown Mall Back on Track After Lawsuit Resolved
articles.summary: Parky's, a planned family entertainment center at Moorestown Mall in New Jersey, is moving forward again after a lawsuit that had delayed the project was settled. The resolution clears the path for one of the region's anticipated new FEC openings in the mall entertainment space. This story is a useful reminder of the legal and permitting risks operators face during development phases.

articles.title: Universal Orlando Launches Unlimited 2-Park Florida Resident Ticket for $199
articles.summary: Universal Orlando Resort has introduced a new Florida resident ticket offering unlimited access to Universal Studios Florida and Universal Islands of Adventure through December 16 for $199 plus tax. The aggressive pricing strategy signals intensifying competition for local repeat visitors in the Central Florida market. FEC operators in the region should be aware of the added pressure this creates on discretionary leisure spending.

articles.title: PortAventura World to Add Two New Family-Focused Zones This Summer
articles.summary: PortAventura World in Spain is expanding its resort this summer with two new themed areas designed specifically for families. The additions reflect a broader European theme park trend toward family-friendly zone development as operators compete to attract multigenerational groups. FEC designers and operators can look to these expansions for inspiration on family zone programming and theming approaches.

articles.title: IAAPA Expo Europe 2026 Achieves Certified Sustainable Event Status
articles.summary: IAAPA Expo Europe 2026 has been certified as a sustainable event following an independent third-party review, scoring 76% against mandatory sustainability requirements. The certification signals the attractions industry's growing commitment to environmental standards at its flagship events. FEC operators attending or exhibiting should be aware of the sustainability focus shaping industry conversations heading into the expo.

articles.title: Michigan's Adventure Celebrates 70th Season with Free Admission for Guests 70 and Older
articles.summary: Michigan's Adventure is marking its 70th season by offering free admission to guests aged 70 and older on select weekdays through its 70 Plus Club promotion. The initiative is a creative community engagement and PR strategy that ties milestone anniversaries to meaningful guest incentives. FEC operators planning milestone anniversaries should consider similar age-matched loyalty promotions to generate goodwill and media attention.
